entry.title: MAD-HTLC
entry.summary:
The discussion revolves around significant concerns regarding the assumptions made about the Bitcoin network, particularly the mempool's functionality. The primary issue raised is the flawed assumption that the mempool is a universally shared resource across the network. In reality, discrepancies can exist between different mempools in various areas of the network for extended periods. Such discrepancies open avenues for potential attacks, as highlighted by the referenced RBF pinning thread. This variability could lead to scenarios where one party, such as Bob, might not be aware of a transaction published by another party, Alice, and might consequently publish his timeout transaction. This action inadvertently provides miners with both preimages, setting up an unhealthy incentive structure.
Furthermore, there is a critical concern regarding the incentives for miners when dealing with Hash Time Locked Contracts (HTLCs). The current setup potentially encourages miners to delay transaction confirmations in hopes of receiving the second preimage, thereby allowing them to claim the funds themselves. This situation represents a departure from the existing norms where miners had no financial gain from delaying confirmations. The email suggests reevaluating these dynamics and invites further exploration into this threat model to better safeguard the network against such vulnerabilities.
For more details on the technical discussions and the specific thread mentioned, refer to the [Linux Foundation mailing list](https://lists.linuxfoundation.org/pipermail/lightning-dev/2020-April/002639.html).
